steps.title: Identify what caused the ecologists' reservations
steps.content: The ecologists “express reservations about the utility” of traditional pollination-syndrome descriptions. Reservations (doubts about usefulness) arise when a theory fails to match reality. The blank must describe the relationship between traditional descriptions and recent empirical observations that triggered this skepticism.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Discrepancies between’: “Discrepancies between traditional descriptions… and recent empirical observations” = contradictions or mismatches between the two — exactly what would make ecologists doubt the descriptions’ usefulness. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Proclamations of’: “Proclamations of traditional descriptions” = confident public announcements of those descriptions; agreement or assertion, not conflict, which would not cause doubt. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Recurrences of’: “Recurrences of traditional descriptions” = the same descriptions appearing repeatedly; repetition would reinforce, not undermine, their credibility. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Affinities between’: “Affinities between traditional descriptions and recent observations” = similarities or agreement between the two; alignment with new data would support, not call into question, the descriptions’ utility. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
